name: TOMATO AND WATERMELON GAZPACHO
intro: with ½ teaspoon chile oil drizzled over each portion.

ingredients.item: lb/2 kg tomatoes (about 20), blanched, peeled, and coarsely chopped (10½ cups/1.9 kg)
celery stalks, white parts and leaves finely chopped (about 3 cups/450 g)
small onion, finely chopped (1 cup/140 g)
cups/400 g seeded and coarsely chopped watermelon flesh
oz/100 g crustless white bread (about 4 slices), broken into small chunks
cup/150 ml passata (tomato purée) or tomato juice
red wine vinegar scant 1 cup/200 ml olive oil, plus extra to finish salt and black pepper coarse sea salt, to serve
oz/150 g crustless white bread (about 5 slices), broken into ¾ to 1¼-inch/2 to 3-cm chunks

method: First make the croutons. Preheat the oven to 400°F/200°C. Place the bread in a bowl along with the oil, vinegar, and ½ teaspoon salt. Place a grill pan over high heat, add the croutons, and cook for 2 minutes, turning until all sides are
slightly charred and starting to crisp. Transfer the croutons to a baking sheet and place in the oven for about 12 minutes, until golden brown and crispy. Set aside to cool.

Place the tomatoes, garlic, celery, onion, watermelon, bread, passata, and two-thirds of the basil in a blender (or large bowl, if using a handheld blender) along with ¾ teaspoon salt and a good grind of black pepper. Blend until smooth and then, with the blender still going, add the vinegar and olive oil. Refrigerate until needed.
To serve, pour the soup into individual bowls and top with the croutons.
Scatter the remaining basil leaves over each portion, along with a final drizzle of oil. Finish with a little coarse salt and serve at once.
